title: ericoides aggregatum
parts: Ericoides aggregatum has many medicinal parts, such as its roots, leaves, flowers, and stems. The roots of the plant are rich in compounds like iridoids and phenylethanoid glycosides, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The leaves of Ericoides aggregatum contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and antiseptic activities. The flowers of the plant are rich in essential oils and glycosides, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions.
side_effects: Ericoides aggregatum can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, allergic reactions, and hormonal imbalances. Ingesting large quantities can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, as well as increased heart rate and blood pressure. Improper use of this plant can also cause skin irritation, including rashes and itching, and may interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners. In severe cases, prolonged exposure to the plant's extracts can lead to liver or kidney damage.
